ClusterInfoList.description: Information on each of the MSK clusters in the response.

NextToken.description: The paginated results marker. When the result of a ListClusters operation is truncated, the call returns NextToken in the response. To get another batch of clusters, provide this token in your next request.

BrokerAZDistribution.description:
The distribution of broker nodes across Availability Zones. This is an optional parameter. If you don't specify it, Amazon MSK gives it the value DEFAULT. You can also explicitly set this parameter to the value DEFAULT. No other values are currently allowed.

Amazon MSK distributes the broker nodes evenly across the Availability Zones that correspond to the subnets you provide when you create the cluster.

ClientSubnets.description: The list of subnets to connect to in the client virtual private cloud (VPC). AWS creates elastic network interfaces inside these subnets. Client applications use elastic network interfaces to produce and consume data. Client subnets can't occupy the Availability Zone with ID use use1-az3.

InstanceType.description: The type of Amazon EC2 instances to use for Apache Kafka brokers. The following instance types are allowed: kafka.m5.large, kafka.m5.xlarge, kafka.m5.2xlarge, kafka.m5.4xlarge, kafka.m5.12xlarge, and kafka.m5.24xlarge.

SecurityGroups.description: The AWS security groups to associate with the elastic network interfaces in order to specify who can connect to and communicate with the Amazon MSK cluster. If you don't specify a security group, Amazon MSK uses the default security group associated with the VPC.

StorageInfo.description: Contains information about storage volumes attached to MSK broker nodes.

VolumeSize.description: The size in GiB of the EBS volume for the data drive on each broker node.

Type.description: The value DISABLED indicates that public access is turned off. SERVICE_PROVIDED_EIPS indicates that public access is turned on.

ClientAuthentication.description: Includes all client authentication information for VPC connectivity.

ZoneIds.description: The list of zoneIds for the cluster in the virtual private cloud (VPC).

Rebalancing.description: Contains information about intelligent rebalancing for new MSK Provisioned clusters with Express brokers. By default, intelligent rebalancing status is ACTIVE.

Status.description: Intelligent rebalancing status. The default intelligent rebalancing status is ACTIVE for all new Express-based clusters.

Enabled.description: SASL/SCRAM authentication is enabled or not.

Iam.description: Indicates whether IAM access control is enabled.

Enabled.description: Indicates whether IAM access control is enabled.
Specifies whether you want to turn on or turn off TLS authentication.

Unauthenticated.description: Contains information about unauthenticated traffic to the cluster.

Enabled.description: Specifies whether you want to turn on or turn off unauthenticated traffic to your cluster.

ClusterArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that uniquely identifies the cluster.

CreationTime.description: The time when the cluster was created.

CurrentBrokerSoftwareInfo.description: Information about the version of software currently deployed on the Apache Kafka brokers in the cluster.

ConfigurationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the configuration used for the cluster. This field isn't visible in this preview release.

ConfigurationRevision.description: The revision of the configuration to use. This field isn't visible in this preview release.

CurrentVersion.description: The current version of the MSK cluster.

DataVolumeKMSKeyId.description: The ARN of the AWS KMS key for encrypting data at rest. If you don't specify a KMS key, MSK creates one for you and uses it.

ClientBroker.description:
Indicates the encryption setting for data in transit between clients and brokers. The following are the possible values.

TLS means that client-broker communication is enabled with TLS only.

TLS_PLAINTEXT means that client-broker communication is enabled for both TLS-encrypted, as well as plaintext data.

PLAINTEXT means that client-broker communication is enabled in plaintext only.

The default value is TLS_PLAINTEXT.

InCluster.description:
When set to true, it indicates that data communication among the broker nodes of the cluster is encrypted. When set to false, the communication happens in plaintext.

The default value is true.

EnhancedMonitoring.description: Specifies which metrics are gathered for the MSK cluster. This property has the following possible values: DEFAULT, PER_BROKER, PER_TOPIC_PER_BROKER, and PER_TOPIC_PER_PARTITION. For a list of the metrics associated with each of these levels of monitoring, see Monitoring.

JmxExporter.description: Indicates whether you want to turn on or turn off the JMX Exporter.

EnabledInBroker.description: Indicates whether you want to turn on or turn off the JMX Exporter.

NodeExporter.description: Indicates whether you want to turn on or turn off the Node Exporter.

EnabledInBroker.description: Indicates whether you want to turn on or turn off the Node Exporter.

Enabled.description: Enabled field
Enabled field
Enabled field

NumberOfBrokerNodes.description: The number of broker nodes in the cluster.

State.description: The state of the cluster. The possible states are ACTIVE, CREATING, DELETING, FAILED, HEALING, MAINTENANCE, REBOOTING_BROKER, and UPDATING.

ZookeeperConnectString.description: The connection string to use to connect to the Apache ZooKeeper cluster.

ZookeeperConnectStringTls.description: The connection string to use to connect to zookeeper cluster on Tls port.

StorageMode.description: This controls storage mode for supported storage tiers.

CustomerActionStatus.description: Determines if there is an action required from the customer.

NextToken.description: The paginated results marker. When the result of a ListClusters operation is truncated, the call returns NextToken in the response. To get another batch of clusters, provide this token in your next request.

ClusterArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that uniquely identifies the cluster.

CreationTime.description: The time when the cluster was created.

CurrentVersion.description: The current version of the MSK cluster.

State.description: The state of the cluster. The possible states are ACTIVE, CREATING, DELETING, FAILED, HEALING, MAINTENANCE, REBOOTING_BROKER, and UPDATING.

StateInfo.description: State Info for the Amazon MSK cluster.

StorageInfo.description: Contains information about storage volumes attached to MSK broker nodes.

ZoneIds.description: The list of zoneIds for the cluster in the virtual private cloud (VPC).

Rebalancing.description: Specifies whether or not intelligent rebalancing is turned on for a newly created MSK Provisioned cluster with Express brokers. Intelligent rebalancing performs automatic partition balancing operations when you scale your clusters up or down. By default, intelligent rebalancing is ACTIVE for all new Express-based clusters.

Status.description: Intelligent rebalancing status. The default intelligent rebalancing status is ACTIVE for all new Express-based clusters.

CurrentBrokerSoftwareInfo.description: Information about the Apache Kafka version deployed on the brokers.

ConfigurationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the configuration used for the cluster. This field isn't visible in this preview release.

ConfigurationRevision.description: The revision of the configuration to use. This field isn't visible in this preview release.

Iam.description: Indicates whether IAM access control is enabled.

Enabled.description: Specifies whether you want to turn on or turn off TLS authentication.

Unauthenticated.description: Contains information about unauthenticated traffic to the cluster.

DataVolumeKMSKeyId.description: The ARN of the AWS KMS key for encrypting data at rest. If you don't specify a KMS key, MSK creates one for you and uses it.

TLS means that client-broker communication is enabled with TLS only.

TLS_PLAINTEXT means that client-broker communication is enabled for both TLS-encrypted, as well as plaintext data.

PLAINTEXT means that client-broker communication is enabled in plaintext only.

The default value is TLS_PLAINTEXT.

InCluster.description:
When set to true, it indicates that data communication among the broker nodes of the cluster is encrypted. When set to false, the communication happens in plaintext.

The default value is true.

EnhancedMonitoring.description: Specifies the level of monitoring for the MSK cluster. The possible values are DEFAULT, PER_BROKER, PER_TOPIC_PER_BROKER, and PER_TOPIC_PER_PARTITION.

JmxExporter.description: Indicates whether you want to turn on or turn off the JMX Exporter.

NodeExporter.description: Indicates whether you want to turn on or turn off the Node Exporter.

NumberOfBrokerNodes.description: The number of broker nodes in the cluster.

ZookeeperConnectString.description: The connection string to use to connect to the Apache ZooKeeper cluster.

ZookeeperConnectStringTls.description: The connection string to use to connect to the Apache ZooKeeper cluster on a TLS port.

StorageMode.description: This controls storage mode for supported storage tiers.

CustomerActionStatus.description: Determines if there is an action required from the customer.

VpcConfigs.description: The configuration of the Amazon VPCs for the cluster.

NextToken.description: The paginated results marker. When the result of a ListConfigurations operation is truncated, the call returns NextToken in the response. To get another batch of configurations, provide this token in your next request.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the configuration.

CreationTime.description: The time when the configuration was created.

KafkaVersions.description: An array of the versions of Apache Kafka with which you can use this MSK configuration. You can use this configuration for an MSK cluster only if the Apache Kafka version specified for the cluster appears in this array.

CreationTime.description: The time when the configuration revision was created.

State.description: The state of the configuration. The possible states are ACTIVE, DELETING, and DELETE_FAILED.

NextToken.description: If the response of ListReplicators is truncated, it returns a NextToken in the response. This NextToken should be sent in the subsequent request to ListReplicators.

Replicators.description: List containing information of each of the replicators in the account.

CreationTime.description: The time the replicator was created.

IsReplicatorReference.description: Whether this resource is a replicator reference.

KafkaClustersSummary.description: Kafka Clusters used in setting up sources / targets for replication.

ReplicationInfoSummaryList.description: A list of summarized information of replications between clusters.

ReplicatorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the replicator.

ReplicatorResourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the replicator resource in the region where the replicator was created.

NextToken.description: The paginated results marker. When the result of a ListClientVpcConnections operation is truncated, the call returns NextToken in the response. To get another batch of configurations, provide this token in your next request.

VpcConnectionArn.description: The ARN that identifies the Vpc Connection.

TargetClusterArn.description: The ARN that identifies the Cluster which the Vpc Connection belongs to.

CreationTime.description: Creation time of the Vpc Connection.

Authentication.description: Information about the auth scheme of Vpc Connection.

VpcId.description: The vpcId that belongs to the Vpc Connection.

UnprocessedScramSecrets.description: List of errors when associating secrets to cluster.

StorageMode.description: This controls storage mode for supported storage tiers.

CreatedBrokerIds.description: Kafka Broker IDs of brokers being created.

DeletedBrokerIds.description: Kafka Broker IDs of brokers being deleted.

Rebalancing.description: Describes the intelligent rebalancing configuration of an MSK Provisioned cluster with Express brokers.

Status.description: Intelligent rebalancing status. The default intelligent rebalancing status is ACTIVE for all new Express-based clusters.

BrokerEBSVolumeInfo.description: Specifies the size of the EBS volume and the ID of the associated broker.

Revision.description: The revision of the configuration to use.

NumberOfBrokerNodes.description: The number of broker nodes in the cluster.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the configuration.

CreationTime.description: The time when the configuration was created.

KafkaVersions.description: The versions of Apache Kafka with which you can use this MSK configuration.

State.description: The state of the configuration. The possible states are ACTIVE, DELETING, and DELETE_FAILED.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the configuration.

CreationTime.description: The time when the configuration was created.

ServerProperties.description: Contents of the server.properties file. When using the API, you must ensure that the contents of the file are base64 encoded. When using the AWS Management Console, the SDK, or the AWS CLI, the contents of server.properties can be in plaintext.

CreationTime.description: The time when the replicator was created.

CurrentVersion.description: The current version number of the replicator.

IsReplicatorReference.description: Whether this resource is a replicator reference.

KafkaClusters.description: Kafka Clusters used in setting up sources / targets for replication.

ReplicationInfoList.description: A list of replication configurations, where each configuration targets a given source cluster to target cluster replication flow.

ReplicatorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the replicator.

ReplicatorResourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the replicator resource in the region where the replicator was created.

ServiceExecutionRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role used by the replicator to access resources in the customer's account (e.g source and target clusters)

StateInfo.description: Details about the state of the replicator.

Tags.description: List of tags attached to the Replicator.

ConsumerGroupsToExclude.description: List of regular expression patterns indicating the consumer groups that should not be replicated.

ConsumerGroupsToReplicate.description: List of regular expression patterns indicating the consumer groups to copy.

DetectAndCopyNewConsumerGroups.description: Enables synchronization of consumer groups to target cluster.

SynchroniseConsumerGroupOffsets.description: Enables synchronization of consumer group offsets to target cluster. The translated offsets will be written to topic __consumer_offsets.

SourceKafkaClusterAlias.description: The alias of the source Kafka cluster.

TargetCompressionType.description: The compression type to use when producing records to target cluster.

TargetKafkaClusterAlias.description: The alias of the target Kafka cluster.

CopyAccessControlListsForTopics.description: Whether to periodically configure remote topic ACLs to match their corresponding upstream topics.

CopyTopicConfigurations.description: Whether to periodically configure remote topics to match their corresponding upstream topics.

DetectAndCopyNewTopics.description: Whether to periodically check for new topics and partitions.

StartingPosition.description: Configuration for specifying the position in the topics to start replicating from.

TopicNameConfiguration.description: Configuration for specifying replicated topic names should be the same as their corresponding upstream topics or prefixed with source cluster alias.

TopicsToExclude.description: List of regular expression patterns indicating the topics that should not be replicated.

TopicsToReplicate.description: List of regular expression patterns indicating the topics to copy.

TopicArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the topic.

TopicName.description: The Kafka topic name of the topic.

Configs.description: Topic configurations encoded as a Base64 string.

Partitions.description: The list of partition information for the topic.

NextToken.description: The paginated results marker. When the result of a DescribeTopicPartitions operation is truncated, the call returns NextToken in the response. To get another batch of configurations, provide this token in your next request.

Leader.description: The leader broker ID for the partition.

Replicas.description: The list of replica broker IDs for the partition.

Isr.description: The list of in-sync replica broker IDs for the partition.

VpcConnectionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that uniquely identifies a MSK VPC connection.

TargetClusterArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that uniquely identifies an MSK cluster.

VpcId.description: The VPC Id for the VPC connection.

Subnets.description: The list of subnets for the VPC connection.

SecurityGroups.description: The list of security groups for the VPC connection.

CreationTime.description: The creation time of the VPC connection.

Tags.description: A map of tags for the VPC connection.

ClusterArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the cluster.

UnprocessedScramSecrets.description: List of errors when disassociating secrets to cluster.

BootstrapBrokerString.description: A string containing one or more hostname:port pairs.

BootstrapBrokerStringTls.description: A string containing one or more DNS names (or IP) and TLS port pairs.

BootstrapBrokerStringSaslScram.description: A string containing one or more DNS names (or IP) and Sasl Scram port pairs.

BootstrapBrokerStringSaslIam.description: A string that contains one or more DNS names (or IP addresses) and SASL IAM port pairs.

BootstrapBrokerStringPublicTls.description: A string containing one or more DNS names (or IP) and TLS port pairs.

BootstrapBrokerStringPublicSaslScram.description: A string containing one or more DNS names (or IP) and Sasl Scram port pairs.

BootstrapBrokerStringPublicSaslIam.description: A string that contains one or more DNS names (or IP addresses) and SASL IAM port pairs.

BootstrapBrokerStringVpcConnectivityTls.description: A string containing one or more DNS names (or IP) and TLS port pairs for VPC connectivity.

BootstrapBrokerStringVpcConnectivitySasl.description: A string containing one or more DNS names (or IP) and SASL/SCRAM port pairs for VPC connectivity.
A string containing one or more DNS names (or IP) and SASL/IAM port pairs for VPC connectivity.

ClusterOperationInfoList.description: An array of cluster operation information objects.

NextToken.description: If the response of ListClusterOperations is truncated, it returns a NextToken in the response. This Nexttoken should be sent in the subsequent request to ListClusterOperations.

ClientRequestId.description: The ID of the API request that triggered this operation.

ClusterArn.description: ARN of the cluster.

CreationTime.description: The time that the operation was created.

EndTime.description: The time at which the operation finished.

ErrorInfo.description: Describes the error if the operation fails.

ErrorString.description: An optional field to provide more details about the error.

SourceClusterInfo.description: Information about cluster attributes before a cluster is updated.

BrokerEBSVolumeInfo.description: Specifies the size of the EBS volume and the ID of the associated broker.

ConfigurationInfo.description: Information about the changes in the configuration of the brokers.

Revision.description: The revision of the configuration to use.

NumberOfBrokerNodes.description: The number of broker nodes in the cluster.

EnhancedMonitoring.description: Specifies which Apache Kafka metrics Amazon MSK gathers and sends to Amazon CloudWatch for this cluster.

JmxExporter.description: Indicates whether you want to turn on or turn off the JMX Exporter.

NodeExporter.description: Indicates whether you want to turn on or turn off the Node Exporter.

LoggingInfo.description: You can configure your MSK cluster to send broker logs to different destination types. This is a container for the configuration details related to broker logs.

InstanceType.description: Information about the Amazon MSK broker type.

Iam.description: Indicates whether IAM access control is enabled.

Enabled.description: Specifies whether you want to turn on or turn off TLS authentication.

Unauthenticated.description: Contains information about unauthenticated traffic to the cluster.

Enabled.description: Specifies whether you want to turn on or turn off unauthenticated traffic to your cluster.

DataVolumeKMSKeyId.description: The ARN of the AWS KMS key for encrypting data at rest. If you don't specify a KMS key, MSK creates one for you and uses it.

ClientBroker.description:
Indicates the encryption setting for data in transit between clients and brokers. The following are the possible values.

TLS means that client-broker communication is enabled with TLS only.

TLS_PLAINTEXT means that client-broker communication is enabled for both TLS-encrypted, as well as plaintext data.

PLAINTEXT means that client-broker communication is enabled in plaintext only.

The default value is TLS_PLAINTEXT.

InCluster.description:
When set to true, it indicates that data communication among the broker nodes of the cluster is encrypted. When set to false, the communication happens in plaintext.

The default value is true.

Type.description: The value DISABLED indicates that public access is turned off. SERVICE_PROVIDED_EIPS indicates that public access is turned on.

ClientAuthentication.description: Includes all client authentication information for VPC connectivity.

StorageMode.description: This controls storage mode for supported storage tiers.
